title: Security Advisory for Go modules
description: Security Advisory for Go modules on GitHub

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.3.0, 4.2.1, and 4.1.2. XSS could occur via the author_link field of a Slack attachment.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18879
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-498j-wxww-j897
items.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost Server is vulnerable to XSS through author_link field in Slack attachments

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.3.0, 4.2.1, and 4.1.2. Knowledge of a session ID allows revoking another user's session.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18878
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/6be8113eb60cf5ddd2dc1c3f4db05cae0c183086
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/8fbbd688ea2466dd0d70e9c07e9703d78f8a19a5
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/affd35071ea155069979fd359726296de8aa6aaf
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h564-6gc2-fcc6

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.3.0, 4.2.1, and 4.1.2. XSS attacks could occur against an OAuth 2.0 allow/deny page.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18877
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9x8x-w6g5-hx4w

items.content_html:
A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in github.com/sirupsen/logrus when using Entry.Writer() to log a single-line payload larger than 64KB without newline characters. Due to limitations in the internal bufio.Scanner, the read fails with "token too long" and the writer pipe is closed, leaving Writer() unusable and causing application unavailability (DoS). This affects versions < 1.8.3, 1.9.0, and 1.9.2. The issue is fixed in 1.8.3, 1.9.1, and 1.9.3+, where the input is chunked and the writer continues to function even if an error is logged.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-65637
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/issues/1370
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/pull/1376
  • https://github.com/mjuanxd/logrus-dos-poc
  • https://github.com/mjuanxd/logrus-dos-poc/blob/main/README.md
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.8.3
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.9.1
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.9.3
  • https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-GOLANG-GITHUBCOMSIRUPSENLOGRUS-5564391
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/commit/6acd903758687c4a3db3c11701e6c414fcf1c1f7
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4f99-4q7p-p3gh
items.title: [github.com/sirupsen/logrus] Logrus is vulnerable to DoS when using Entry.Writer()

items.content_html:
A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in github.com/sirupsen/logrus when using Entry.Writer() to log a single-line payload larger than 64KB without newline characters. Due to limitations in the internal bufio.Scanner, the read fails with "token too long" and the writer pipe is closed, leaving Writer() unusable and causing application unavailability (DoS). This affects versions < 1.8.3, 1.9.0, and 1.9.2. The issue is fixed in 1.8.3, 1.9.1, and 1.9.3+, where the input is chunked and the writer continues to function even if an error is logged.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-65637
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/issues/1370
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/pull/1376
  • https://github.com/mjuanxd/logrus-dos-poc
  • https://github.com/mjuanxd/logrus-dos-poc/blob/main/README.md
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.8.3
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.9.1
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/releases/tag/v1.9.3
  • https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-GOLANG-GITHUBCOMSIRUPSENLOGRUS-5564391
  • https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/commit/6acd903758687c4a3db3c11701e6c414fcf1c1f7
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4f99-4q7p-p3gh

A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in github.com/sirupsen/logrus when using Entry.Writer() to log a single-line payload larger than 64KB without newline characters. Due to limitations in the internal bufio.Scanner, the read fails with "token too long" and the writer pipe is closed, leaving Writer() unusable and causing application unavailability (DoS). This affects versions < 1.8.3, 1.9.0, and 1.9.2. The issue is fixed in 1.8.3, 1.9.1, and 1.9.3+, where the input is chunked and the writer continues to function even if an error is logged.

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.3.0, 4.2.1, and 4.1.2 when local storage for files is used. A System Admin can test for the existence of an arbitrary file.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18876
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/6be8113eb60cf5ddd2dc1c3f4db05cae0c183086
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/6d3cb2ce07fc799832081e93843b405b390057fa
  • https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/commit/fadd9514f6e71590aba781a7035e1de4150137b0
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hjqh-j6rj-gh8q
items.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost Server is vulnerable to Path Traversal when files are stored locally

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.1.0, 4.0.4, and 3.10.3. It allows attackers to discover team invite IDs via team API endpoints.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18902
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jwfv-5hwq-f97r
items.title: [github.com/mattermost/mattermost-server] Mattermost Server exposes team invite IDs through API endpoints

items.content_html:
An issue was discovered in Mattermost Server before 4.1.0, 4.0.4, and 3.10.3. It allows attackers to discover team invite IDs via team API endpoints.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18902
  • https://mattermost.com/security-updates
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jwfv-5hwq-f97r
